title: Sita Samahit Sthal
tagline: Sita Samahit Sthal — spiritual town of Uttar Pradesh

overview.short: Sita Samahit Sthal (Sitamarhi), the holy place of Sitamarhi is situated between Prayagraj and Varanasi, near the national highway No.
overview.description: Sita Samahit Sthal (Sitamarhi), the holy place of Sitamarhi is situated between Prayagraj and Varanasi, near the national highway No. 2 and also connected with Prayagraj and Varanasi railway line with Jangiganj, the nearest railway station. It is a well known Hindu pilgrimage in the poorvanchal region of Uttar Pradesh .

topPlaces.name: Nagurda
topPlaces.description: Nagurda is a village in Belgaum district of Karnataka, India.

topPlaces.name: Itiha Ibrahimpur
topPlaces.description: Itiha Ibrahimpur is a village in Handia Tehsil in Prayagraj District of Uttar Pradesh, India, situated on the Ganges river. It belongs to Prayagraj division.

topPlaces.name: Nibi Gaharwar
topPlaces.description: Nibi Gaharwar is a village in the Mirzapur district of India. The Ganges flows on one side of the village, with Chanvar fields on the other.

topPlaces.name: Deegh
topPlaces.description: Deegh is a town and a Block (Mandal) in Bhadohi district in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh.

topPlaces.name: Konia Kshetra
topPlaces.description: Konia Kshetra is a region of Bhadohi district, Uttar Pradesh, India. The Ganges River flows through its west end and changes its direction at Checuwan - Bhurra village, where the river's turbulence is reduced.

items.note: Nagurda is a village in Belgaum district of Karnataka, India.
Itiha Ibrahimpur is a village in Handia Tehsil in Prayagraj District of Uttar Pradesh, India, situated on the Ganges river. It belongs to Prayagraj division.
Nibi Gaharwar is a village in the Mirzapur district of India. The Ganges flows on one side of the village, with Chanvar fields on the other.
Deegh is a town and a Block (Mandal) in Bhadohi district in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh.

Konia Kshetra is a region of Bhadohi district, Uttar Pradesh, India. The Ganges River flows through its west end and changes its direction at Checuwan - Bhurra village, where the river's turbulence is reduced.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Sita Samahit Sthal?
faq.a: The best time to visit Sita Samahit Sthal is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Sita Samahit Sthal?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Lal Bahadur Shastri Airport, Varanasi (58 km) and the nearest railway station is Prayagraj Junction (56 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Sita Samahit Sthal?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Sita Samahit Sthal at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Sita Samahit Sthal?
faq.a: Stays in Sita Samahit Sthal start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Sita Samahit Sthal?
faq.a: The top attractions are Nagurda, Itiha Ibrahimpur, Nibi Gaharwar, Deegh, Konia Kshetra.
